For the rest of the year he gives only two useful bearings. On one, he is too close to Fort Fisher and the Mound Batter.
Most of the time, he leaves his position at night, cruises around a bit and drifts, then returns in the morning.
Hardly ever does he record any data about these trips. I can do nothing, but leave him in this position, unless
I get some indications of his movements.
Sometimes he logs Blockading off Wilmington and other times he's off New Inlet. I take these as equivalent.

He logs no anchorage time. He usually comes in between 8 and 10. Moving him back to his starting point.
His courses and distances put him well offshore. Given his 15.4 mile move to the east, he can't be in his "usual" position.
I decided that his last two courses are reversed. Using WxS instead of NxE puts him back in his usual place.
